sections.label: Macro
sections.body: A relief bounce, breadth jumped to 14/20 green on flat BTC, alt-led by XLM and HYPE. The base is fragile: BTC ETF outflows hit a record nine straight days at $2.8B, sentiment is stuck in Extreme Fear, and the risk-on tone leans on unconfirmed Iran truce-extension headlines. One green day on a weekly structure that is still broken, majors down 5% on the week.

sections.label: Perps
sections.body: Chop across the board, zero regime breakouts on 25 assets, all neutral. XLM is the standout, breaking out +28% on volume 22.8x its norm with shorts liquidated at 53x the weekly average. HYPE reclaimed +6.7% toward its $64.27 all-time high, but top-trader long/short is still falling. No clean new setup to ride beyond what is already on the book.

sections.label: Book
sections.body: Six positions, closing two. The BCH short hits its seven-day horizon at +13% as the legacy-alt bleed ran clean to the end. The TAO long closes on a thesis break. It fell -6.9% on a green day, inverting the relative-strength tell that justified the hold, now below every major moving average. Riding XLM at +20%, plus SEI, EIGEN, ICP. Adding one new long in INJ on relative strength.

market_sentiment.bias_line: Bias · ride the book, bank the BCH win and take the TAO loss. One new entry in INJ on relative strength, otherwise patient. The bounce is a day old on record ETF outflows — let XLM run and watch HYPE for the ATH break.

current_positions.thesis_note: Seven-day horizon reached with the short at +13%. The legacy-alt bleed ran clean to the end, BCH down another 10.5% today even on a green relief tape, funding negative and smart money fully exited.
Down 3% from entry on day 6 of 7, the AI-compute momentum leg broken but the EigenCloud build structural.
current_positions.watch: Sector momentum has broken, but the $0.205 stop sits 8% below price. A daily close under $0.205 ends it early, otherwise it closes on horizon tomorrow.

current_positions.thesis_note: Closing on a thesis break. TAO fell -6.9% on a green relief day, inverting the relative-strength tell that held the position, and sits below every major moving average with the narrative cooling. Wicked $254.67 intraday under the $255 stop, held the close above it.
Deepest-underwater hold at -10%, bounced +2.7% today to sit 3% above the $2.65 stop. The supply-deflation thesis is structural and multi-week, not a daily trade.
current_positions.watch: Sits 3% above the $2.65 stop after grazing it intraday. The MISSION70 deflation thesis runs multi-week, so a single red day does not break it. Only a daily close below $2.65 ends it.

current_positions.thesis_note: Bounced +5.4% today back to near flat from entry, the L1 name participating in the relief while compute lagged. The June 15 EVM migration is the dated catalyst ahead.
current_positions.watch: Bounced +5.4% today, holding 10% clear of the $0.062 stop. The June 15 EVM migration is the catalyst the multi-week thesis waits on. A daily close below $0.062 ends it before then.

current_positions.thesis_note: Up ~20% from entry on day 2 of 3. The settlement-narrative breakout extended +28% on volume 22.8x its norm with short liquidations 53x the weekly average.
current_positions.watch: Up ~20% and running on real volume. Watch for the squeeze maturing. Open interest unwinding or top traders flipping net long signals the fuel is spent. A close back below $0.152 unwinds the breakout.

thesis: Injective ran +12% on the day and +11% on the week, outpacing red majors by ~16 points over seven days — relative strength, not a one-day relief pop.
Turnover is the heaviest on the board at a third of market cap — $199M of volume on a $600M mid-cap, real flow crossing into the move.
The catalyst is developer-and-policy, not a dated event: the Nova program with Microsoft and Azure launched May 25, native EVM mainnet is live, a Policy Institute opened in Washington.
It leads the AI-token rotation, the one name there with fresh accelerating momentum while NEAR and the compute names cool.
risks: The bid is rotational and narrative-driven. If AI-token attention rotates out, INJ fades fast with the cohort.
The book already holds two AI-adjacent longs in EIGEN and ICP. A sector unwind hits INJ alongside them rather than diversifying.
The move sits on a one-day relief bounce with BTC ETF outflows at a record nine days and sentiment in Extreme Fear. A flush drags momentum names down regardless of catalyst.

watchlist.trigger: daily close above the $64.27 all-time high with volume expanding above the recent average
watchlist.invalidation: close below $58 before the trigger fires
thesis: Hyperliquid reclaimed +6.7% on the day back toward its $64.27 all-time high, confirming the green-flip after last week's failed bounce.
The institutional case stacked hard this week. CME launched 24/7 crypto futures, Grayscale filed a Hyperliquid ETF, and DEX volume ran $7.01B, up 11.6%.
Top-trader long/short is still falling, down 0.26 over seven days. Smart money hasn't joined the reclaim yet, so this waits for a clean ATH break rather than a chase here.

watchlist.trigger: second daily close below $560 with funding flipping positive past +0.02%/8h AND top-trader long/short rotating above 1.0
watchlist.invalidation: close above $620 before the trigger fires
thesis: Zcash bled -17% on the week and tests $500. The privacy narrative is fading with drivers collapsed to a single mention, the token leading the move down.
Funding sits negative at -0.0164%/8h with top traders net short at 0.88. The short side is already crowded, so the trigger waits for that to clear before adding.
The institutional floor is intact — Grayscale's ZCSH ETF and the late-2026 Tachyon roadmap. That floor is the un-fade risk if ZEC re-anchors above $560.

evaluations.note: Day 7/7. BCH printed in today's 25-asset perps slice at $299.29 down -10.5% 24h, funding negative at -0.0346%/8h and top L/S collapsed to 0.99 — the legacy-alt bleed ran clean through to horizon even on a 14/20-green relief tape. Seven-day horizon reached with the short at +13.17%. Invalidation $370 never approached. CLOSE, WIN.

Day 6/7. EIGEN ~$0.223 (CoinGecko/CMC consensus) down ~3% from the $0.2299 entry — absent from the perps slice and token-movers, so price carries estimate uncertainty. The AI-compute momentum leg has broken, but EIGEN holds an 8% cushion to the $0.205 stop and the EigenCloud/Nebius build is structural. Horizon expires tomorrow. RIDE.

Day 4/7. TAO ~$260.50 down -6.9% 24h on a green relief day — the relative-strength tell that justified the hold (held while peers fell on 05-28) inverted today, and TAO now sits below every major moving average with the narrative demoted and smart money trimming. Wicked $254.67 intraday under the $255 stop but held the close above it, so no breach flag. Closing on the thesis break into the flush, not on the stop, consistent with the AKT/RENDER closes on 05-28. -6.39% return. CLOSE, LOSS.

Day 3/multi-week. ICP $2.72 bounced +2.7% today (CMC) but absent from the perps slice — price carries estimate uncertainty, the intraday low near $2.65 grazed the stop without a close below. Deepest-underwater hold at -9.9%, sitting 3% above the $2.65 stop. The MISSION70 supply-deflation thesis is structural and multi-week, not a daily trade. RIDE on tight watch.

Day 3/multi-week. SEI $0.06906 up +5.4% 24h (CoinGecko) — the L1 name participated in the relief while compute lagged, now near flat from the $0.0697 entry with the $0.062 stop 10% below. The June 15 EVM migration is the dated catalyst. The best intraday print tagged the entry level, lifting MFE just green. RIDE.

Day 2/3. XLM ~$0.207 (CoinGecko spot; perps mark $0.210) up +28% 24h, +43% 7d — the settlement-narrative breakout extended on volume 22.8x its norm with short liquidations 53x the weekly average. Up ~20% from the $0.1731 entry, first eval since fire so MAE/MFE initialize today. Invalidation $0.152 well clear. RIDE the winner.

close.close_reason: seven-day horizon reached with the short at +13.17%; the legacy-alt structural bleed ran clean to the end, BCH down another 10.5% today even on a green relief tape with funding negative and smart money fully exited

thesis break on day 4/7 — TAO fell -6.9% on a green relief day, inverting the relative-strength tell that justified the hold, now below every major moving average with the decentralized-compute narrative cooling and smart money trimming; wicked $254.67 intraday under the $255 stop but held the close above it, closing on the thesis break into the flush rather than riding to the stop, consistent with the AKT/RENDER closes on 05-28

The catalyst is developer-and-policy, not a dated event: the Nova program with Microsoft and Azure launched May 25, native EVM mainnet is live, a Policy Institute opened in Washington.

engine_watch_conditions.trigger_label: longs paying premium — crowding into the move
smart money crowded long — distribution risk
open interest building — fresh leverage entering

add_watchlist.invalidation: close below $58 before the trigger fires
thesis: Hyperliquid reclaimed +6.7% on the day back toward its $64.27 all-time high, confirming the green-flip after last week's failed bounce.
The institutional case stacked hard this week. CME launched 24/7 crypto futures, Grayscale filed a Hyperliquid ETF, and DEX volume ran $7.01B, up 11.6%.
Top-trader long/short is still falling, down 0.26 over seven days. Smart money hasn't joined the reclaim yet, so this waits for a clean ATH break rather than a chase here.
named_risks: We closed HYPE neutral three days ago on a fade-peak-fade whipsaw, so re-entering before the ATH break confirms risks chasing the same chop.
Smart-money long/short is still falling, so the reclaim is retail-led until that turns up.
